title: Davis Craft and Vintage Fair
location: Central Park – Davis, 5th St & B St, Davis, CA, 95616, United States
description:
Davis Craft and Vintage Fair began as the Square Tomatoes Craft Fair more than 10 years ago! The event was started by instructors from the UC Davis Craft Center in order to have a monthly craft fair for local artisans and the local community. We embraced local vintage vendors in 2017, we’ve grown and we are still home to local artists and makers from the Davis and greater Sacramento area. 
We collaborate with local non-profits starting with Couleurs Vives Art Studio who joined us in 2018! Many of our talented vendors can be found at other festivals like the Davis Phoenix Coalition’s Pride Fest, the I-House’s International Festival, and 2nd Friday ArtAbouts on E Street Plaza. 
Expect to see friendly vendors with local handmade and original art, crafts, quality vintage items, upcycled treasures, earth-friendly creations, good food, and more. Free live music fills the open space for a lively atmosphere so be sure to stop by and join us!

title: Art in Bloom
location: Pence Gallery, 212 D Street, Davis, CA, 95616, United States
description:
Art in Bloom 
Dates/times: March 13, 6-9 PM, March 14-15, 11:30 AM – 5 PM
Location: Pence Gallery, 212 D Street, Davis, CA 95616
Admission: Admission to the Pence is free; we welcome donations from visitors, as they help us cover basic costs associated with running a nonprofit art gallery. 
Art in Bloom is the first collaboration with members of the Davis Flower Arrangers and the Pence Gallery, in which creative floral arrangements are paired with specific artworks on view at the Pence from March 13-15. On Friday, March 13, from 6-9 PM, enjoy meeting many of the participating floral designers and see the arrangements as they are at their freshest, as part of the city’s 2nd Friday ArtAbout reception. Visitors are welcome to enjoy a variety of arrangements that include traditional and unusual materials, and a variety of beautiful containers. The connection between the artwork and floral design can include using similar colors, themes, textures, or overall composition. 
Participating floral designers include Janet Goehring, Joyce Hamaguchi, Elly Fairclough, MJ Kelly, Rhody Vallejo, Elisabetta Vivoda, Mike MacDonald, Stephanie DeGraff-Hunt, and Julie Haney. Each artist has chosen an artwork for inspiration, and will create a vibrant interpretation of pieces by exhibiting artists Manuel Fernando Rios, Barbara Prodaniuk, and artists from Assorted Flavors, our annual exhibit of artworks created by forty Pence members. 
Founded in 1958, the Davis Flower Arrangers is a long-standing organization in Davis, dedicated to studying and instructing floral design techniques, including Ikebana. The group, celebrating its 67th season in 2025-26, promotes the public appreciation of floral art through workshops, demonstrations, and community events. For more information on the Davis Flower Arrangers, contact President Stephanie DeGraff-Hunt at sdegraffhunt@gmail.com. 
Image: Davis Flower Arrangement (past event)

title: Davis High School Presents Urinetown—The Musical!
location: Richard Brunelle Performance Hall, 315 W 14th Street, Davis, CA, 95616, United States
description:
Urinetown: The Musical 
Set in a grim, Gotham-like city ravaged by a 20-year drought, Urinetown imagines a world where private toilets are outlawed. Citizens must pay to use public restrooms, all controlled by a single, corrupt corporation that profits off one of humanity’s most basic needs. When an unlikely hero decides he’s had enough, a rebellion is born—one that challenges authority, exposes hypocrisy, and asks what freedom really costs. 
Hilariously funny and touchingly human, Urinetown reexamines the very notion of what a musical can be, proving that even in the darkest circumstances, revolution can begin with a laugh—or a pee. 
Location
Brunelle Theater @ 315 W 14th Street. 
Performances
Friday March 6, Saturday March 7, Friday March 13, Saturday March 14 @7:00PM
Sunday March 8 and Sunday March 15 @2:00PM 
Tickets
$15 general admission, $10 for DJUSD students, and $5 on Sundays for DJUSD students. 
You can buy your tickets online at https://sites.google.com/view/davishightheatre/home or at the door. We hope to see you there!

title: Little Shop of Horrors
location: Davis Musical Theatre Co. (DMTC), 607 Pena Dr.
description:
Little Shop of Horrors – March 6 through March 29, 2026
Underwritten by Sharon Joyce
The meek floral assistant Seymour Krelborn stumbles across a new breed of plant he names “Audrey II” – after his coworker crush. This foul-mouthed, R&B-singing carnivore promises unending fame and fortune to the down and out Krelborn as long as he keeps feeding it, BLOOD. Over time, though, Seymour discovers Audrey II’s out of this world origins and intent towards global domination! 
Directed by Steve Isaacson
Choreographed by Logan Sexton
Musical direction by Tonya Whitson-Whennen

title: Introduction to Remote Computing and HPC (2-day workshop)
location: Shields Library, 360
description:
Are you working on a research project but finding that your data is too big for your laptop, or that your code must run for a long time? You need more compute power!
 

This 2-day intensive workshop provides an introduction to accessing and computing on remote servers such as UC Davis’ HPC (high performance cluster) to elevate your research. 
 

The series covers everything you need to know to get started. We’ll provide an overview of HPC terminology, architecture, and general workflows, and guide learners through how to set up and use SSH to log in and transfer files, how to install software with miniconda, how to reserve computing time and run programs with SLURM, and execute shell commands that are especially useful for working with servers. As we learn we’ll practice accessing and computing on remote servers using the UC Davis HPC Core Facility’s new “Hive” cluster.
 

The material builds and thus learners must attend both days. Sessions run from 10 AM - 4 PM each day, with a break for lunch.
 

After this workshop series, learners should be able to:

Use SSH to log in to a server;Transfer files to and from a server;Set up and use conda/mamba to install software on a server;Use SLURM to run interactive and non-interactive software on a server;Explain etiquette for using a server cluster such as Hive.
This workshop is subject to cancellation if minimum enrollment is not achieved.
 

PREREQUISITES

Participants should have taken DataLab’s “Introduction to the Unix Command Line” workshop, or have equivalent prior experience using command line. Specifically, you will need to know how to use command line to navigate your file system and inspect, create, edit and move files. Participants must be comfortable with programming in a language such as R or Python, and with basic Linux shell syntax (i.e., able to navigate and create, modify and move files via command line).
 

Learners will register for a HPC account during the session. CAS authentication is required.


Can't make it to this training? Check out our workshop schedule. Materials from prior workshops are available on DataLab's website.
 

Questions? Email datalab-training@ucdavis.edu.

title: Davis R Users' Group
location: Shields Library, 360
description:
D-RUG, our local R Users' Group, meets weekly to facilitate learning and using the R software language. R users from all domains are invited to bring their R-specific coding problems and share their experience with other members of the community. Meetup formats include everything from group discussion and mini-workshops to coworking sessions. D-RUG is run by volunteer Coo[R]dinators including UC Davis data scientists and graduate students dedicated to growing and strengthening the R community.

 

Today's session is open-forum format to foster a communal, co-working environment. Come hang out and chat, work on your own, seek and give help, and make friends.

 

D-RUG meets Thursdays in person from 10 AM to 12 PM at the DataLab Classroom (Shields Library room 360) on the UC Davis campus. 

Sign up for our mailing list to get the most up to date information, including a Zoom link to join us virtually if you can't make it in person. Learn more about D-RUG on our website.

title: Weekly Game Night at Blue Note
description:
Yolo County Board Game Gathering
Update 8/14/25:
**We're still active**, but we primarily use Discord to chat and plan events. (See group description for invite link.)

We still meet every Wednesday for board games at Blue Note Brewing Company. We'll have a handful of tables reserved near the back; just look for the pile of board games.

People usually start arriving around 5pm, and games start whenever people are ready. Blue Note closes at 9pm, and although we have a 5-10 minute grace period there, you should be in the process of final scoring by 9, or else pack it up and play again another day.

Game night is open to all ages! Blue Note is a brewery, not a bar, so there's no restriction against minors. In addition to their crafted-in-house beer selection, water and sodas are available.

You're not required to buy anything in order to join us, but we do encourage you to support local businesses. Blue Note's chef team makes a mean pizza, and there are other nearby downtown restaurants and businesses.

title: NAMI Connection Support Group for Young Adults - Virtual
location: CA
description:
NAMI Connection Support Group for Young Adults is a free, peer-led support group for anyone between the ages 18 and 26 who has experienced symptoms of a mental health condition. Mental health conditions include anxiety disorder, depression, bipolar disorder, obsessive-compulsive disorder, post-traumatic stress disorder, schizophrenia, or any condition that affects a person’s ability to lead a fulfilling life. You do not need a formal diagnosis to attend this support group; any young person concerned with their mental health and seeking support may attend. NAMI Connection for Young Adults provides resources to help young people build a support network and access help. Participants will learn together how to establish and maintain wellness. 
You do not need a formal diagnosis to attend. Anyone who is seeking mental health support may attend. 
What You’ll Gain
By sharing your experiences in a safe and confidential setting, you will experience connection, receive information about resources, and learn coping strategies. The group encourages empathy, productive discussion and a sense of community. You’ll benefit from other’s experiences, discover your inner strength, and receive support by sharing your own experiences in a non-judgmental space. 
  
NAMI Connection Support Group for Young Adults meets on the first Wednesday of the month (in person) and on the third Wednesday of the month  (via Zoom) from 7pm-8pm.  
To register for in-person or virtual meetings, please visit: https://namiyolo.org/support-and-education/nami-yolo-county-support-groups/young-adult-connection-support-group/. 
If you have any questions, please email: friends@namiyolo.org.

Beetlejuice Jr – March 14 through April 5, 2026

It’s our show now, kid. The ghost-with-the-most comes to the stage in Beetlejuice JR., the frightfully funny musical based on the beloved motion picture turned Broadway phenomenon. Lydia Deetz is a strange and unusual teenager, still grieving the loss of her mother and obsessed with the whole “being dead thing.” Lucky for Lydia, she and her father move to a new house haunted by a recently deceased couple and Beetlejuice, a delightful demon with a real zest for life. When Lydia calls on Beetlejuice to scare away anyone with a pulse, this double-crossing specter shows his true stripes, unleashing a (Nether)world of pandemonium. 
Recommended for ages 10 & up 
  
Directed by Tea Pusey
Choreographed by Logan Sexton
Musical direction by Montana Monce

title: Intermediate Watercolor Painting with Misuk Goltz (6-Sessions)
description:
Pence Adult Art Programs
In this 6-week workshop, students will create watercolor paintings inspired by Misuk’s recent travels to Italy, Croatia, Montenegro, Greece, and Turkey. Using these Mediterranean image references, participants will explore landscape, architectural, and figurative subjects rich in light, atmosphere and color. Students will focus on designing harmonious compositions, developing value studies for each subject, and working with limited color palettes to achieve cohesive, painterly results. For this workshop, participants should have prior experience with watercolor techniques and color blending.

title: Weekly Game Night at Blue Note
description:
Yolo County Board Game Gathering
Update 8/14/25:
**We're still active**, but we primarily use Discord to chat and plan events. (See group description for invite link.)

We still meet every Wednesday for board games at Blue Note Brewing Company. We'll have a handful of tables reserved near the back; just look for the pile of board games.

People usually start arriving around 5pm, and games start whenever people are ready. Blue Note closes at 9pm, and although we have a 5-10 minute grace period there, you should be in the process of final scoring by 9, or else pack it up and play again another day.

Game night is open to all ages! Blue Note is a brewery, not a bar, so there's no restriction against minors. In addition to their crafted-in-house beer selection, water and sodas are available.

You're not required to buy anything in order to join us, but we do encourage you to support local businesses. Blue Note's chef team makes a mean pizza, and there are other nearby downtown restaurants and businesses.

title: The Pence Joins Yolo Fest This Spring!
location: 212 D Street, Davis, CA 95616
description:
Community Event

The Pence Joins Yolo Fest This Spring!

The Pence Gallery, a unique nonprofit art gallery highlighting local & regional artists, is participating in the county-wide Yolo Fest this spring. From March 27-29, 2026, Yolo Fest celebrates the region’s agriculture, music, arts, culture, farm-to-table food, craft beverages, recreation, and local hospitality. 

The Pence Gallery in Davis will be open with three spectacular art exhibits from 11:30 AM - 5 PM, and admission is free to view these displays by these visual artists:

Manuel Fernando Rios: When They Reminisce Over You

February 7 - March 29

In his exhibit, Rios shares his interest in themes that explore his Chicano heritage, as well as his family’s history and experiences. Blurring the line between naturalism and abstraction, the artist embraces the use of complex design patterns, photography, and text to make a powerful statement. 

Assorted Flavors: Pence Member Exhibit

March 13 - April 16

View 40 works of art created by Pence Members in this widely ranging annual display of paintings, photographs, textiles, and more. 

Barbara Prodaniuk: Myths in Mud

March 13 - April 26

Prodaniuk is a ceramic artist whose work is inspired by the forest, meadows, and waterways of the Truckee area. She explores stories carried by the animals and artifacts found in these environments. 

Special Weekend Activities

Self-guided Scavenger Hunt:

March 28-29, 11:30 AM - 4:30 PM

Free to participate!

Join us for a fun, art-themed scavenger hunt at the gallery by exploring the Pence’s exhibits. Complete all of the clues for the chance to be entered in a raffle to win a $25 gift certificate to the Pence gift store! A raffle winner will be selected and contacted at 4:30 PM on both Saturday and Sunday.

Printmaking for All Ages

Sunday, March 29, 1-3 PM

$5 Members | $10 Non-Members (per person, all materials included)

In this workshop, printmaking is made easy! Learn how to create bold and colorful block prints using a Styrofoam plate, printmaking ink, and a few simple tricks and techniques. This is a drop-in program for all ages, and no sign-ups are required in advance. Children ages 5 and up may participate if they are accompanied by an adult.

title: A Guided Nature Walk (Free event)
description:
Iama Embodied Community
Join Michael for a guided exploration into the practice of slowing down and deepening our relationship with the natural world. This immersive walk invites participants to engage with the subtle intelligence of the landscape, from Blue Oaks and seasonal wildflowers to the presence of scrub jays overhead. Together, we will explore how to “read” trees, interpret the wind, and sit in quiet reflection with grandmother trees. Along the way, we will open conversation about how nature teaches, communicates, and invites us into deeper awareness.

**Location:** Sterling Pointe Ct, Loomis, CA 95650
**Distance:** 3.2 miles
**Elevation Gain:** 375 feet
**Level:** Easy to Moderate
**Pace:** Slow, with frequent stops for observation and discussion
**Meet at trailhead:** 9:30 AM
**Hike Begins:** 9:45 AM
**End Time:** 1:15 PM

Please bring lunch, water, and appropriate footwear.
This experience is designed not as a workout, but as a practice in presence.

### About the guide

Michael is a lifelong hiker, backpacker, and outdoor enthusiast. He is WFR-trained and adult CPR-certified. Michael’s connection to the land has been shaped by journeys through places like Death Valley and the Grand Canyon, though his true roots run deep in Mount Diablo. Guided by curiosity and a love of self-studying trees and plants, he has learned the languages of wind, water, and earth—finding meaning in slowing down and walking with presence.

**P.S.** If you wish to carpool from Sacramento, meet at Parkview Presbyterian Church’s parking lot on 727 T st at 8:30 AM. We will be leaving at 8:45 sharp.

Get ready for an unforgettable evening where food and wine truly speak the same language.
Join us at Great Bear Vineyards for an elegant, immersive dining experience featuring a six-course, chef-crafted menu, each dish thoughtfully paired with our estate-grown wines. description:
Wine & Cheese Pairing Experience at Great Bear Vineyards in Davis Sunday, March 29 | 12:00–3:00 pm
If wine and cheese are your love language, this one’s for you.
Join us at Great Bear Vineyards for an afternoon of delicious discovery featuring a curated flight of four award-winning wines, each thoughtfully paired with a premium artisan cheese designed to bring out the very best in both. It’s indulgent, flavorful, and dangerously easy to fall in love with every pairing.
 Want to elevate your experience?Add an exclusive behind-the-scenes barrel room tour, where you’ll see our winemaking process up close and sample estate-grown wines straight from the barrel—a rare and memorable treat.
This experience is perfect whether you’re a devoted foodie, a curious wine lover, or simply in the mood for something delightfully decadent. You’ll get the chance to:• Savor the unique profiles of our handcrafted wines• Discover why certain cheeses make wines shine• Pick up fun, easy pairing tips—or just relax and enjoy every bite and sip
Bring your favorite people and let the flavors unfold. The pairing offers a hearty, satisfying tasting with a focus on flavor harmony. Additional wine and snacks will be available for purchase if you’d like to linger a little longer.

title: Collaborative Data Science Office Hours (virtual)
description:
DataLab and colleagues from research computing and statistical service units across UC Davis offer weekly free virtual office hours during which UC Davis researchers can obtain help and advice on working with their data. During these short, just-in-time sessions we meet with faculty, students, postdoctoral scholars, and research staff from all domains across the University to discuss their research challenges.

Register in advance for the Zoom link. This helps us connect you to the right expert for your question.

In addition to DataLab, the collaborative data science office hours are supported by:

Biostatistics (Clinical and Translational Science Center, CTSC)Computational Research Data Service (College of Letters & Science)High Performance Computing (HPC) CoreResearcher Services (UC Davis Library)Stat Lab (Department of Statistics)Common office hours help topics include:

Open Source Programming (R, Python, Julia, etc.)Reproducible research computing and data scienceResponsible and ethical data scienceData gathering, organization, databases, and experimental designData analysis, statistics, and explorationData visualizationMachine learning and neural networksText mining and natural language processingGeospatial sciencesRemote and high performance computingComputational pedagogy… and more!Please note that drop-in office hours are not offered during University holidays, academic breaks and finals week. Summer schedule varies based on availability.

Note: Office hours are intended for short "just in time" research support. If you require extensive assistance we may refer you a recharge service, upcoming funding/project calls, and/or other resources. We are unable to provide support for assigned coursework (homework assignments, class projects, etc.) during office hours. If you need such help please contact your instructor or TA for assistance. If you need help with technical problems such system administration and hardware issues, check out the the UC Davis ServiceHub and/or contact your departmental IT.

title: Davis R Users' Group

description:
D-RUG, our local R Users' Group, meets weekly to facilitate learning and using the R software language. R users from all domains are invited to bring their R-specific coding problems and share their experience with other members of the community. Meetup formats include everything from group discussion and mini-workshops to coworking sessions. D-RUG is run by volunteer Coo[R]dinators including UC Davis data scientists and graduate students dedicated to growing and strengthening the R community.

 

Today's session is open-forum format to foster a communal, co-working environment. Come hang out and chat, work on your own, seek and give help, and make friends.

 

D-RUG meets Thursdays in person from 10 AM to 12 PM at the DataLab Classroom (Shields Library room 360) on the UC Davis campus. 

Sign up for our mailing list to get the most up to date information, including a Zoom link to join us virtually if you can't make it in person. Learn more about D-RUG on our website.

title: NAMI Connection Support Group for Young Adults
location: CA
description:
NAMI Connection Support Group for Young Adults is a free, peer-led support group for anyone between the ages 18 and 26 who has experienced symptoms of a mental health condition. Mental health conditions include anxiety disorder, depression, bipolar disorder, obsessive-compulsive disorder, post-traumatic stress disorder, schizophrenia, or any condition that affects a person’s ability to lead a fulfilling life. You do not need a formal diagnosis to attend this support group; any young person concerned with their mental health and seeking support may attend. NAMI Connection for Young Adults provides resources to help young people build a support network and access help. Participants will learn together how to establish and maintain wellness. 
You do not need a formal diagnosis to attend. Anyone who is seeking mental health support may attend. 
What You’ll Gain
By sharing your experiences in a safe and confidential setting, you will experience connection, receive information about resources, and learn coping strategies. The group encourages empathy, productive discussion and a sense of community. You’ll benefit from other’s experiences, discover your inner strength, and receive support by sharing your own experiences in a non-judgmental space. 
  
NAMI Connection Support Group for Young Adults meets on the first Wednesday of the month (in person) and on the third Wednesday of the month  (via Zoom) from 7pm-8pm.  
To register for in-person or virtual meetings, please visit: https://namiyolo.org/support-and-education/nami-yolo-county-support-groups/young-adult-connection-support-group/. 
If you have any questions, please email: friends@namiyolo.org.

title: Collaborative Data Science Office Hours (virtual)
description:
DataLab and colleagues from research computing and statistical service units across UC Davis offer weekly free virtual office hours during which UC Davis researchers can obtain help and advice on working with their data. During these short, just-in-time sessions we meet with faculty, students, postdoctoral scholars, and research staff from all domains across the University to discuss their research challenges.

Register in advance for the Zoom link. This helps us connect you to the right expert for your question.

In addition to DataLab, the collaborative data science office hours are supported by:

Biostatistics (Clinical and Translational Science Center, CTSC)Computational Research Data Service (College of Letters & Science)High Performance Computing (HPC) CoreResearcher Services (UC Davis Library)Stat Lab (Department of Statistics)Common office hours help topics include:

Open Source Programming (R, Python, Julia, etc.)Reproducible research computing and data scienceResponsible and ethical data scienceData gathering, organization, databases, and experimental designData analysis, statistics, and explorationData visualizationMachine learning and neural networksText mining and natural language processingGeospatial sciencesRemote and high performance computingComputational pedagogy… and more!Please note that drop-in office hours are not offered during University holidays, academic breaks and finals week. Summer schedule varies based on availability.

Note: Office hours are intended for short "just in time" research support. If you require extensive assistance we may refer you a recharge service, upcoming funding/project calls, and/or other resources. We are unable to provide support for assigned coursework (homework assignments, class projects, etc.) during office hours. If you need such help please contact your instructor or TA for assistance. If you need help with technical problems such system administration and hardware issues, check out the the UC Davis ServiceHub and/or contact your departmental IT.

title: Davis R Users' Group
location: Shields Library, 360
description:
D-RUG, our local R Users' Group, meets weekly to facilitate learning and using the R software language. R users from all domains are invited to bring their R-specific coding problems and share their experience with other members of the community. Meetup formats include everything from group discussion and mini-workshops to coworking sessions. D-RUG is run by volunteer Coo[R]dinators including UC Davis data scientists and graduate students dedicated to growing and strengthening the R community.

 

Today's session is open-forum format to foster a communal, co-working environment. Come hang out and chat, work on your own, seek and give help, and make friends.

 

D-RUG meets Thursdays in person from 10 AM to 12 PM at the DataLab Classroom (Shields Library room 360) on the UC Davis campus. 

Sign up for our mailing list to get the most up to date information, including a Zoom link to join us virtually if you can't make it in person. Learn more about D-RUG on our website.

title: Weekly Game Night at Blue Note
description:
Yolo County Board Game Gathering
Update 8/14/25:
**We're still active**, but we primarily use Discord to chat and plan events. (See group description for invite link.)

We still meet every Wednesday for board games at Blue Note Brewing Company. We'll have a handful of tables reserved near the back; just look for the pile of board games.

People usually start arriving around 5pm, and games start whenever people are ready. Blue Note closes at 9pm, and although we have a 5-10 minute grace period there, you should be in the process of final scoring by 9, or else pack it up and play again another day.

Game night is open to all ages! Blue Note is a brewery, not a bar, so there's no restriction against minors. In addition to their crafted-in-house beer selection, water and sodas are available.

You're not required to buy anything in order to join us, but we do encourage you to support local businesses. Blue Note's chef team makes a mean pizza, and there are other nearby downtown restaurants and businesses.

title: Creative Writing Series: Maria Kuznetsova, Fiction
location: Shields Library, 205
description:
Please join us for the next event in the 2025-2026 Creative Writing Series: a reading and Q&A featuring novelist Maria Kuznetsova!

This event is free and open to all, and will be available in person and livestreamed.

 

Maria Kuznetsova was born in Kyiv, Ukraine, and moved to the United States as a child. She is the author of the novels Oksana, Behave! and Something Unbelievable. An alum of the UC Davis master’s program in creative writing, she now lives in Auburn, Alabama, with her family, where she is an associate professor of creative writing at Auburn University. She is also the fiction editor of the Southern Humanities Review and her work appears or is forthcoming in dozens of literary journals including One Story, The Yale Review, Southern Review, Threepenny Review, and The Sun.

 

The Creative Writing Series is presented by the UC Davis Department of English, Creative Writing Master of Fine Arts Program, and the UC Davis Library.

The views expressed in this series, including descriptions and biographies, are those of the speakers and not meant to represent the views of any other person or institution.

title: Publish Your Work: From Idea to Impact — Traditional and Alternative Options
description:
Publish Your Work: From Idea to Impact, Traditional and Alternative Options is a Zoom workshop that will give you an overview of (1) traditional publishing options, such as peer-reviewed journals, and (2) alternative publishing options, such as preprints, open methods, and open data.

Whether you’re going to be publishing for the first time or you’ve published many times in the past, join us to learn more about open research, avoiding predatory publishers, selecting an appropriate journal, and how to publish your research outputs in ways that go beyond the traditional journal article.
 

No previous experience is required. All disciplines are welcome.

Please register for the Zoom link. 

This six-workshop series is a collaboration between the library's Scholarly Communications Program, the Researcher Services department, and Blaisdell Medical Library. For any questions, please contact the Scholarly Communication Officer Michael Ladisch (mladisch@ucdavis.edu). Check our events calendar for more publishing workshops.

title: AI Literacy & Logic: 1-day intensive course
description:
A 198 course for College of Letters & Science majors.

The landscape of the academic and professional world is shifting. Whether you are an aspiring historian, a budding chemist, or a future sociologist, AI is no longer a "tech" skill—it’s a foundational literacy.

Developed through a collaboration between the UC Davis College of Letters & Science and DataLab, this 1-unit intensive course is designed to unveil Artificial Intelligence. We’ve stripped away the dense math and complex coding to give you a conceptual, high-level understanding of how artificial intelligence systems work, why they matter, and how to make them work for you.

Why Enroll?

In a single day, you will move from being a passive AI user to a critical, strategic collaborator. This training is specifically curated for L&S students to ensure you graduate with the competitive edge needed for the rapidly evolving, AI-driven workplace.

No technical background? No problem. This course has no prerequisites. It is designed for thinkers, writers, and researchers across all liberal arts and science disciplines.

The curriculum is divided into four deep-dive modules:

How AI Thinks
Understand the "brain" of the machine. We’ll explore the theories of language and cognition that allow Large Language Models (LLMs) to function, and provide a framework for evaluating their potential and limits.

How to Use AI Responsibly
AI isn't a magic wand—it’s a tool with a history and consequences. We discuss the environmental and societal impacts, how to ensure the veracity of AI results, and why reproducibility is the cornerstone of ethical tech.

How to Talk with AI
Master the art and science of prompt engineering. Learn what happens behind the scenes when you send a prompt. Develop sophisticated strategies to get the exact results you need.

Module 4: How AI Talks with You
A look at the future of AI implementation. From simple chatbots to complex autonomous "Agents," learn how AI is being embedded into systems and how to evaluate its activity.

By the end of this one-day course, you will be able to:

Define "AI" and distinguish it from traditional statistics and data science.

Identify whether a real-world problem is suitable for an AI solution.

Develop advanced prompting strategies that produce reliable, useful results.

Critically interrogate your own engagement with AI and its impact on our society and environment.

 

Apply Now

This intensive, one-day course will be offered five times during Spring Quarter 2026. You only need to signup for one date. 

Time: 9:00 AM – 4:30 PM (with a break for lunch)

Credits: 1 Unit

CRN & location: Will be emailed to accepted and confirmed registrants.

Eligibility: Open to all students with a major in the College of Letters & Science.

Priority: Registration priority is given to Seniors (4th years) and students graduating in Spring or Summer 2026. Other students in the College of Letters and Science are encouraged to register and will be admitted as space permits.

title: Collaborative Data Science Office Hours (virtual)
description:
DataLab and colleagues from research computing and statistical service units across UC Davis offer weekly free virtual office hours during which UC Davis researchers can obtain help and advice on working with their data. During these short, just-in-time sessions we meet with faculty, students, postdoctoral scholars, and research staff from all domains across the University to discuss their research challenges.

Register in advance for the Zoom link. This helps us connect you to the right expert for your question.

In addition to DataLab, the collaborative data science office hours are supported by:

Biostatistics (Clinical and Translational Science Center, CTSC)Computational Research Data Service (College of Letters & Science)High Performance Computing (HPC) CoreResearcher Services (UC Davis Library)Stat Lab (Department of Statistics)Common office hours help topics include:

Open Source Programming (R, Python, Julia, etc.)Reproducible research computing and data scienceResponsible and ethical data scienceData gathering, organization, databases, and experimental designData analysis, statistics, and explorationData visualizationMachine learning and neural networksText mining and natural language processingGeospatial sciencesRemote and high performance computingComputational pedagogy… and more!Please note that drop-in office hours are not offered during University holidays, academic breaks and finals week. Summer schedule varies based on availability.

Note: Office hours are intended for short "just in time" research support. title: Covered: Faculty Author Talks by Lisa G. Materson and Corrie Decker
location: Manetti Shrem Museum
description:
Join us for an evening with UC Davis faculty authors Lisa G. Materson and Corrie Decker as part of Covered, a monthly event series celebrating recent books from the College of Letters and Science.
 

Lisa G. Materson, associate professor of history, traces civil rights activist Ruth Reynolds’ deep involvement in the movement for Puerto Rico’s independence in Radical Solidarity, offering a nuanced account of political allyship, anti-colonial struggle and cross-racial collaboration. 
 Corrie Decker, associate professor of history, examines how colonial courts and ethnographers in 20th century East Africa transformed complex rites of passage into simplified “puberty rites" in The Age of Sex: Custom, Law, and Ritual in Twentieth-Century East Africa, reshaping legal understandings of childhood, adulthood, gender and sexual maturity. 
 

Covered: Spotlighting Faculty Authors in Letters and Science is co-sponsored by the Jan Shrem and Maria Manetti Shrem Museum and the UC Davis Library.

description:
D-RUG, our local R Users' Group, meets weekly to facilitate learning and using the R software language. R users from all domains are invited to bring their R-specific coding problems and share their experience with other members of the community. Meetup formats include everything from group discussion and mini-workshops to coworking sessions. D-RUG is run by volunteer Coo[R]dinators including UC Davis data scientists and graduate students dedicated to growing and strengthening the R community.

 

Today's session is open-forum format to foster a communal, co-working environment. Come hang out and chat, work on your own, seek and give help, and make friends.

 

D-RUG meets Thursdays in person from 10 AM to 12 PM at the DataLab Classroom (Shields Library room 360) on the UC Davis campus. 

Sign up for our mailing list to get the most up to date information, including a Zoom link to join us virtually if you can't make it in person. Learn more about D-RUG on our website.

Common office hours help topics include:

Open Source Programming (R, Python, Julia, etc.)Reproducible research computing and data scienceResponsible and ethical data scienceData gathering, organization, databases, and experimental designData analysis, statistics, and explorationData visualizationMachine learning and neural networksText mining and natural language processingGeospatial sciencesRemote and high performance computingComputational pedagogy… and more!Please note that drop-in office hours are not offered during University holidays, academic breaks and finals week. Summer schedule varies. DataLab may only be accessed during Shields Library's business hours. Click here for detailed directions to DataLab.

title: Weekly Game Night at Blue Note
description:
Yolo County Board Game Gathering
Update 8/14/25:
**We're still active**, but we primarily use Discord to chat and plan events. (See group description for invite link.)

We still meet every Wednesday for board games at Blue Note Brewing Company. We'll have a handful of tables reserved near the back; just look for the pile of board games.

People usually start arriving around 5pm, and games start whenever people are ready. Blue Note closes at 9pm, and although we have a 5-10 minute grace period there, you should be in the process of final scoring by 9, or else pack it up and play again another day.

Game night is open to all ages! Blue Note is a brewery, not a bar, so there's no restriction against minors. In addition to their crafted-in-house beer selection, water and sodas are available.

You're not required to buy anything in order to join us, but we do encourage you to support local businesses. Blue Note's chef team makes a mean pizza, and there are other nearby downtown restaurants and businesses.

title: NAMI Connection Support Group for Young Adults - Virtual
location: CA
description:
NAMI Connection Support Group for Young Adults is a free, peer-led support group for anyone between the ages 18 and 26 who has experienced symptoms of a mental health condition. Mental health conditions include anxiety disorder, depression, bipolar disorder, obsessive-compulsive disorder, post-traumatic stress disorder, schizophrenia, or any condition that affects a person’s ability to lead a fulfilling life. You do not need a formal diagnosis to attend this support group; any young person concerned with their mental health and seeking support may attend. NAMI Connection for Young Adults provides resources to help young people build a support network and access help. Participants will learn together how to establish and maintain wellness. 
You do not need a formal diagnosis to attend. Anyone who is seeking mental health support may attend. 
What You’ll Gain
By sharing your experiences in a safe and confidential setting, you will experience connection, receive information about resources, and learn coping strategies. The group encourages empathy, productive discussion and a sense of community. You’ll benefit from other’s experiences, discover your inner strength, and receive support by sharing your own experiences in a non-judgmental space. 
  
NAMI Connection Support Group for Young Adults meets on the first Wednesday of the month (in person) and on the third Wednesday of the month  (via Zoom) from 7pm-8pm.  
To register for in-person or virtual meetings, please visit: https://namiyolo.org/support-and-education/nami-yolo-county-support-groups/young-adult-connection-support-group/. 
If you have any questions, please email: friends@namiyolo.org.

title: From Idea to Impact: Publishing Your Work — Demystifying the Manuscript Submission Process
description:
Publishing research in an academic journal is a critical step in every scholar’s journey. However, it can be a very complex and intimidating landscape, especially for those who have limited experience with the process. This workshop will provide an overview of the manuscript submission process including, manuscript preparation, guidance on citing AI tools, the peer review process, and how to go about choosing copyright terms when uploading your paper.
 

No previous experience is required. All disciplines are welcome. 

Please register for the Zoom link.

This six-workshop series is a collaboration between the library's Scholarly Communications Program, the Researcher Services department, and Blaisdell Medical Library. For any questions, please contact the Scholarly Communication Officer Michael Ladisch (mladisch@ucdavis.edu). Check our events calendar for more publishing workshops.

title: Creative Writing Series: Summer Farah, Poetry
location: Shields Library, 205
description:
Please join us for the next event in the 2025-2026 Creative Writing Series: a poetry reading and Q&A featuring poet, essayist and zine-maker Summer Farah!

This event is free and open to all, and will be available in person and livestreamed.
 

Summer Farah is a Palestinian American writer, editor, and zine-maker from California. She is the author of The Hungering Years (Host Publications, 2026) and the chapbook I could die today and live again (Game Over Books, 2024). Her essay "From Witness From Speech From Image: On Etel Adnan’s The Arab Apocalypse and Theresa Hak Kyung Cha’s Dictee" received a 2025 Krause Essay Prize honorable mention. Summer is a member of the Radius of Arab American Writers and the National Book Critics Circle. She is calling on you to recommit yourself to the liberation of the Palestinian people each day.
 

This event is co-sponsored with the Department of Middle East/South Asia Studies.
 

The Creative Writing Series is presented by the UC Davis Department of English, Creative Writing Master of Fine Arts Program,
and the UC Davis Library.

The views expressed in this series, including descriptions and biographies, are those of the speakers and not meant to represent the views of any other person or institution.

title: Art in Action: Woven Fairy House Wall Hangings
description:
Pence Family Art Workshops
Our fairy houses are back – with a twist! Learn how to make a simple loom with sticks and twine, and weave flowers, leaves, and other natural materials to create your very own wall hanging. Decorate your woven art piece with moss, fabric butterflies, beads, and more to create the perfect fairy hideaway.

Art in Action: Woven Fairy House Wall Hangings
Sunday, April 19, 1-3 PM
$5 Members, $10 Non-Members (per person, all materials included)

**How it works:**
Art in Action engages kids in art projects that are fun, creative, and collaborative with their family members. This is a drop-in program for ages 5-12. No registration in advance is required, but space and supplies are limited to the first 25 participants and are available on a first come, first served basis.

When you arrive, pay with cash or card at the front desk. A parent or adult caregiver is required to stay, but only has to pay for the project for themselves if they are also doing art. Stay as long or as short as you like over the two hour period.

Become a Member starting at $70/year and save! If you join the Pence Gallery when you attend this workshop, you can enjoy Member pricing for Art in Action, plus receive a $5 gift certificate to our gift shop as a thank you. Membership supports art for all ages and makes creative moments like this possible—jump in and be part of it.

We have a different Art in Action family workshop every month! Learn more at [https://pencegallery.org/art-in-action/](https://pencegallery.org/art-in-action/).

This unique monthly program is generously sponsored and made possible by Mary Stephens DeWall and Les DeWall.

title: Collaborative Data Science Office Hours (virtual)
description:
DataLab and colleagues from research computing and statistical service units across UC Davis offer weekly free virtual office hours during which UC Davis researchers can obtain help and advice on working with their data. During these short, just-in-time sessions we meet with faculty, students, postdoctoral scholars, and research staff from all domains across the University to discuss their research challenges.

Register in advance for the Zoom link. This helps us connect you to the right expert for your question.

In addition to DataLab, the collaborative data science office hours are supported by:

Biostatistics (Clinical and Translational Science Center, CTSC)Computational Research Data Service (College of Letters & Science)High Performance Computing (HPC) CoreResearcher Services (UC Davis Library)Stat Lab (Department of Statistics)Common office hours help topics include:

Open Source Programming (R, Python, Julia, etc.)Reproducible research computing and data scienceResponsible and ethical data scienceData gathering, organization, databases, and experimental designData analysis, statistics, and explorationData visualizationMachine learning and neural networksText mining and natural language processingGeospatial sciencesRemote and high performance computingComputational pedagogy… and more!Please note that drop-in office hours are not offered during University holidays, academic breaks and finals week. Summer schedule varies based on availability.

Note: Office hours are intended for short "just in time" research support. If you require extensive assistance we may refer you a recharge service, upcoming funding/project calls, and/or other resources. We are unable to provide support for assigned coursework (homework assignments, class projects, etc.) during office hours. If you need such help please contact your instructor or TA for assistance. If you need help with technical problems such system administration and hardware issues, check out the the UC Davis ServiceHub and/or contact your departmental IT.

title: Davis R Users' Group
location: Shields Library, 360
description:
D-RUG, our local R Users' Group, meets weekly to facilitate learning and using the R software language. R users from all domains are invited to bring their R-specific coding problems and share their experience with other members of the community. Meetup formats include everything from group discussion and mini-workshops to coworking sessions. D-RUG is run by volunteer Coo[R]dinators including UC Davis data scientists and graduate students dedicated to growing and strengthening the R community.

 

Today's session is open-forum format to foster a communal, co-working environment. Come hang out and chat, work on your own, seek and give help, and make friends.

 

D-RUG meets Thursdays in person from 10 AM to 12 PM at the DataLab Classroom (Shields Library room 360) on the UC Davis campus. 

Sign up for our mailing list to get the most up to date information, including a Zoom link to join us virtually if you can't make it in person. Learn more about D-RUG on our website.

title: From Idea to Impact: Publishing Your Work — Open Access Publishing: Mandates, Policies, Support
description:
Maximize your research impact and global reach by publishing Open Access. In this workshop we will show you how to use UC’s transformative agreements and other library initiatives to have the Library cover your publishing fees, ensuring your work is freely available from day one. We will briefly address staying compliant with federal public access mandates, and provide a short overview of the UC Open Access Policies.
 

No previous experience is required. All disciplines are welcome. 

Please register for the Zoom link.

This six-workshop series is a collaboration between the library's Scholarly Communications Program, the Researcher Services department, and Blaisdell Medical Library. For any questions, please contact the Scholarly Communication Officer Michael Ladisch (mladisch@ucdavis.edu). Check our events calendar for more publishing workshops.

description:
Davis Art, Music & Outdoor Activities Meetup
**Discover Intercultural Mosaics This Winter 2025–2026!**

Join us for a vibrant and uplifting season of virtual learning and community from **December 20, 2025 to March 28, 2026**—all from the comfort of your home on Zoom!

**Registration is required, and the Zoom link will be provided exclusively to those who sign up.**

**Bi-Weekly Friday Zoom Sessions** *(Every 2nd and 4th Friday)*
• **4:00 PM** – Advanced Korean with Stephen
• **5:00 PM** – Intermediate Conversational Chinese with Sam
• **6:00 PM** – Bible Study & Meditation Group with Stephen
• **7:00 PM** – Beginning Chinese with Jane

**Weekly Saturday Zoom Sessions** (total of 12 sessions)
• **4:00 PM** – Beginning Spanish with Viviana
• **5:00 PM** – Beginning to Intermediate Korean with Stephen
• **6:00 PM** – Beginning to Intermediate Japanese with Norio
• **7:00 PM** – English Learners Group with Linda & Chad

**Important Notes:**

1. **No Zoom sessions** on **January 2–3, 2026** (New Year) and **April 3–4, 2026** (Easter).
2. **Special Gatherings (Tentative):**
**• February 14, 2026:** Valentine’s Day & pre–Lunar New Year celebration in person at Phoenix Hall, 2nd Floor, Davis Community Church.
**• March 14, 2026:** Additional in-person gathering (tentative).

**Return to In-Person Meetings:**
We will resume our **regular in-person gatherings on April 11, 2026** at the **2nd floor rooms of Phoenix Hall, Davis Community Church**.

For more information about Intercultural Mosaics classes and activities, visit **[www.nextg.org](http://www.nextg.org)** or contact **Dr. Stephen Moon** at **916-217-5470** or **catalyst@nextg.org**.

title: Weekly Game Night at Blue Note
description:
Yolo County Board Game Gathering
Update 8/14/25:
**We're still active**, but we primarily use Discord to chat and plan events. (See group description for invite link.)

We still meet every Wednesday for board games at Blue Note Brewing Company. We'll have a handful of tables reserved near the back; just look for the pile of board games.

People usually start arriving around 5pm, and games start whenever people are ready. Blue Note closes at 9pm, and although we have a 5-10 minute grace period there, you should be in the process of final scoring by 9, or else pack it up and play again another day.

Game night is open to all ages! Blue Note is a brewery, not a bar, so there's no restriction against minors. In addition to their crafted-in-house beer selection, water and sodas are available.

You're not required to buy anything in order to join us, but we do encourage you to support local businesses. Blue Note's chef team makes a mean pizza, and there are other nearby downtown restaurants and businesses.

title: From Idea to Impact: Publishing Your Work — Navigating Data Management for Publication
description:
This workshop is targeted toward scholars, researchers, and graduate students who are interested in the role of data management during the publication process. Managing your data effectively is a crucial part of research and scholarly communication. This session will provide foundational skills for handling data throughout your project's lifecycle, from planning for funder requirements to ensuring long-term reproducibility.
 

After this workshop, learners should be able to:
 

Understand and navigate funder requirements for data management and sharing.Use the Data Management Planning (DMP) Tool to organize research projects.Apply fundamental data management strategies during the active execution phase of research.Protect sensitive or personal data if collected during researchDistinguish between working data storage and open data repositories (platforms used for sharing and archiving final data).Locate the appropriate data repository for your specific discipline or project.Explain the broader importance of data reproducibility in the research ecosystem.Identify campus resources available for data support, including the DataLab, Research Data Management (RDM) Team, and the Clinical and Translational Science Center (CTSC).
No previous experience is required. All disciplines are welcome.


Please register for the Zoom link.

This six-workshop series is a collaboration between the library's Scholarly Communications Program, the Researcher Services department, and Blaisdell Medical Library. For any questions, please contact the Scholarly Communication Officer Michael Ladisch (mladisch@ucdavis.edu). Check our events calendar for more publishing workshops.

title: AI Literacy & Logic: 1-day intensive course
description:
A 198 course for College of Letters & Science majors.

The landscape of the academic and professional world is shifting. Whether you are an aspiring historian, a budding chemist, or a future sociologist, AI is no longer a "tech" skill—it’s a foundational literacy.

Developed through a collaboration between the UC Davis College of Letters & Science and DataLab, this 1-unit intensive course is designed to unveil Artificial Intelligence. We’ve stripped away the dense math and complex coding to give you a conceptual, high-level understanding of how artificial intelligence systems work, why they matter, and how to make them work for you.

Why Enroll?

In a single day, you will move from being a passive AI user to a critical, strategic collaborator. This training is specifically curated for L&S students to ensure you graduate with the competitive edge needed for the rapidly evolving, AI-driven workplace.

No technical background? No problem. This course has no prerequisites. It is designed for thinkers, writers, and researchers across all liberal arts and science disciplines.

The curriculum is divided into four deep-dive modules:

How AI Thinks
Understand the "brain" of the machine. We’ll explore the theories of language and cognition that allow Large Language Models (LLMs) to function, and provide a framework for evaluating their potential and limits.

How to Use AI Responsibly
AI isn't a magic wand—it’s a tool with a history and consequences. We discuss the environmental and societal impacts, how to ensure the veracity of AI results, and why reproducibility is the cornerstone of ethical tech.

How to Talk with AI
Master the art and science of prompt engineering. Learn what happens behind the scenes when you send a prompt. Develop sophisticated strategies to get the exact results you need.

Module 4: How AI Talks with You
A look at the future of AI implementation. From simple chatbots to complex autonomous "Agents," learn how AI is being embedded into systems and how to evaluate its activity.

By the end of this one-day course, you will be able to:

Define "AI" and distinguish it from traditional statistics and data science.

Identify whether a real-world problem is suitable for an AI solution.

Develop advanced prompting strategies that produce reliable, useful results.

Critically interrogate your own engagement with AI and its impact on our society and environment.

 

Apply Now

This intensive, one-day course will be offered five times during Spring Quarter 2026. You only need to signup for one date. 

Time: 9:00 AM – 4:30 PM (with a break for lunch)

Credits: 1 Unit

CRN & location: Will be emailed to accepted and confirmed registrants.

Eligibility: Open to all students with a major in the College of Letters & Science.

Priority: Registration priority is given to Seniors (4th years) and students graduating in Spring or Summer 2026. Other students in the College of Letters and Science are encouraged to register and will be admitted as space permits.

title: Collaborative Data Science Office Hours (virtual)
description:
DataLab and colleagues from research computing and statistical service units across UC Davis offer weekly free virtual office hours during which UC Davis researchers can obtain help and advice on working with their data. During these short, just-in-time sessions we meet with faculty, students, postdoctoral scholars, and research staff from all domains across the University to discuss their research challenges.

Register in advance for the Zoom link. This helps us connect you to the right expert for your question.

In addition to DataLab, the collaborative data science office hours are supported by:

Biostatistics (Clinical and Translational Science Center, CTSC)Computational Research Data Service (College of Letters & Science)High Performance Computing (HPC) CoreResearcher Services (UC Davis Library)Stat Lab (Department of Statistics)Common office hours help topics include:

Open Source Programming (R, Python, Julia, etc.)Reproducible research computing and data scienceResponsible and ethical data scienceData gathering, organization, databases, and experimental designData analysis, statistics, and explorationData visualizationMachine learning and neural networksText mining and natural language processingGeospatial sciencesRemote and high performance computingComputational pedagogy… and more!Please note that drop-in office hours are not offered during University holidays, academic breaks and finals week. Summer schedule varies based on availability.

Note: Office hours are intended for short "just in time" research support. If you require extensive assistance we may refer you a recharge service, upcoming funding/project calls, and/or other resources. We are unable to provide support for assigned coursework (homework assignments, class projects, etc.) during office hours. If you need such help please contact your instructor or TA for assistance. If you need help with technical problems such system administration and hardware issues, check out the the UC Davis ServiceHub and/or contact your departmental IT.

title: Davis R Users' Group
location: Shields Library, 360
description:
D-RUG, our local R Users' Group, meets weekly to facilitate learning and using the R software language. R users from all domains are invited to bring their R-specific coding problems and share their experience with other members of the community. Meetup formats include everything from group discussion and mini-workshops to coworking sessions. D-RUG is run by volunteer Coo[R]dinators including UC Davis data scientists and graduate students dedicated to growing and strengthening the R community.

 

Today's session is open-forum format to foster a communal, co-working environment. Come hang out and chat, work on your own, seek and give help, and make friends.

 

D-RUG meets Thursdays in person from 10 AM to 12 PM at the DataLab Classroom (Shields Library room 360) on the UC Davis campus. 

Sign up for our mailing list to get the most up to date information, including a Zoom link to join us virtually if you can't make it in person. Learn more about D-RUG on our website.

title: NAMI Connection Support Group for Young Adults
location: CA
description:
NAMI Connection Support Group for Young Adults is a free, peer-led support group for anyone between the ages 18 and 26 who has experienced symptoms of a mental health condition. Mental health conditions include anxiety disorder, depression, bipolar disorder, obsessive-compulsive disorder, post-traumatic stress disorder, schizophrenia, or any condition that affects a person’s ability to lead a fulfilling life. You do not need a formal diagnosis to attend this support group; any young person concerned with their mental health and seeking support may attend. NAMI Connection for Young Adults provides resources to help young people build a support network and access help. Participants will learn together how to establish and maintain wellness. 
You do not need a formal diagnosis to attend. Anyone who is seeking mental health support may attend. 
What You’ll Gain
By sharing your experiences in a safe and confidential setting, you will experience connection, receive information about resources, and learn coping strategies. The group encourages empathy, productive discussion and a sense of community. You’ll benefit from other’s experiences, discover your inner strength, and receive support by sharing your own experiences in a non-judgmental space. 
  
NAMI Connection Support Group for Young Adults meets on the first Wednesday of the month (in person) and on the third Wednesday of the month  (via Zoom) from 7pm-8pm.  
To register for in-person or virtual meetings, please visit: https://namiyolo.org/support-and-education/nami-yolo-county-support-groups/young-adult-connection-support-group/. 
If you have any questions, please email: friends@namiyolo.org.

title: From Idea to Impact: Publishing Your Work — Copyright and Other Legal Issues
description:
This workshop provides information on owning your work, from understanding how to retain copyright in your first publications to navigating the fine print of author agreements. We will explore how to safely incorporate third-party copyrighted material into your publication and how to strategically use Creative Commons licenses to dictate how others cite and share your findings.
 

No previous experience is required. All disciplines are welcome.


Please register for the Zoom link.

This six-workshop series is a collaboration between the library's Scholarly Communications Program, the Researcher Services department, and Blaisdell Medical Library. For any questions, please contact the Scholarly Communication Officer Michael Ladisch (mladisch@ucdavis.edu). Check our events calendar for more publishing workshops.

title: Collaborative Data Science Office Hours (virtual)
description:
DataLab and colleagues from research computing and statistical service units across UC Davis offer weekly free virtual office hours during which UC Davis researchers can obtain help and advice on working with their data. During these short, just-in-time sessions we meet with faculty, students, postdoctoral scholars, and research staff from all domains across the University to discuss their research challenges.

Register in advance for the Zoom link. This helps us connect you to the right expert for your question.

In addition to DataLab, the collaborative data science office hours are supported by:

Biostatistics (Clinical and Translational Science Center, CTSC)Computational Research Data Service (College of Letters & Science)High Performance Computing (HPC) CoreResearcher Services (UC Davis Library)Stat Lab (Department of Statistics)Common office hours help topics include:

Open Source Programming (R, Python, Julia, etc.)Reproducible research computing and data scienceResponsible and ethical data scienceData gathering, organization, databases, and experimental designData analysis, statistics, and explorationData visualizationMachine learning and neural networksText mining and natural language processingGeospatial sciencesRemote and high performance computingComputational pedagogy… and more!Please note that drop-in office hours are not offered during University holidays, academic breaks and finals week. Summer schedule varies based on availability.

Note: Office hours are intended for short "just in time" research support. If you require extensive assistance we may refer you a recharge service, upcoming funding/project calls, and/or other resources. We are unable to provide support for assigned coursework (homework assignments, class projects, etc.) during office hours. If you need such help please contact your instructor or TA for assistance. If you need help with technical problems such system administration and hardware issues, check out the the UC Davis ServiceHub and/or contact your departmental IT.

title: Davis R Users' Group
location: Shields Library, 360
description:
D-RUG, our local R Users' Group, meets weekly to facilitate learning and using the R software language. R users from all domains are invited to bring their R-specific coding problems and share their experience with other members of the community. Meetup formats include everything from group discussion and mini-workshops to coworking sessions. D-RUG is run by volunteer Coo[R]dinators including UC Davis data scientists and graduate students dedicated to growing and strengthening the R community.

 

Today's session is open-forum format to foster a communal, co-working environment. Come hang out and chat, work on your own, seek and give help, and make friends.

 

D-RUG meets Thursdays in person from 10 AM to 12 PM at the DataLab Classroom (Shields Library room 360) on the UC Davis campus. 

Sign up for our mailing list to get the most up to date information, including a Zoom link to join us virtually if you can't make it in person. Learn more about D-RUG on our website.

title: Covered: Faculty Author Talks by Claire Goldstein and Michael Dylan Foster
location: Manetti Shrem Museum
description:
Join us for an evening with UC Davis faculty authors Claire Goldstein and Michael Dylan Foster as part of Covered, a monthly event series celebrating recent books from the College of Letters and Science.
 

Claire Goldstein, professor of French and Italian, discusses In the Sun King’s Cosmos, her engaging study of how comets shaped scientific, political and cultural imagination in 17th century France. 
 Michael Dylan Foster, professor of East Asian Languages and Cultures, presents the expanded edition of The Book of Yokai, exploring the folklore, history and ongoing cultural life of Japan’s supernatural beings. 



 

Covered: Spotlighting Faculty Authors in Letters and Science is co-sponsored by the Jan Shrem and Maria Manetti Shrem Museum and the UC Davis Library.

title: AI Literacy & Logic: 1-day intensive course
description:
A 198 course for College of Letters & Science majors.

The landscape of the academic and professional world is shifting. Whether you are an aspiring historian, a budding chemist, or a future sociologist, AI is no longer a "tech" skill—it’s a foundational literacy.

Developed through a collaboration between the UC Davis College of Letters & Science and DataLab, this 1-unit intensive course is designed to unveil Artificial Intelligence. We’ve stripped away the dense math and complex coding to give you a conceptual, high-level understanding of how artificial intelligence systems work, why they matter, and how to make them work for you.

Why Enroll?

In a single day, you will move from being a passive AI user to a critical, strategic collaborator. This training is specifically curated for L&S students to ensure you graduate with the competitive edge needed for the rapidly evolving, AI-driven workplace.

No technical background? No problem. This course has no prerequisites. It is designed for thinkers, writers, and researchers across all liberal arts and science disciplines.

The curriculum is divided into four deep-dive modules:

How AI Thinks
Understand the "brain" of the machine. We’ll explore the theories of language and cognition that allow Large Language Models (LLMs) to function, and provide a framework for evaluating their potential and limits.

How to Use AI Responsibly
AI isn't a magic wand—it’s a tool with a history and consequences. We discuss the environmental and societal impacts, how to ensure the veracity of AI results, and why reproducibility is the cornerstone of ethical tech.

How to Talk with AI
Master the art and science of prompt engineering. Learn what happens behind the scenes when you send a prompt. Develop sophisticated strategies to get the exact results you need.

Module 4: How AI Talks with You
A look at the future of AI implementation. From simple chatbots to complex autonomous "Agents," learn how AI is being embedded into systems and how to evaluate its activity.

By the end of this one-day course, you will be able to:

Define "AI" and distinguish it from traditional statistics and data science.

Identify whether a real-world problem is suitable for an AI solution.

Develop advanced prompting strategies that produce reliable, useful results.

Critically interrogate your own engagement with AI and its impact on our society and environment.

 

Apply Now

This intensive, one-day course will be offered five times during Spring Quarter 2026. You only need to signup for one date. 

Time: 9:00 AM – 4:30 PM (with a break for lunch)

Credits: 1 Unit

CRN & location: Will be emailed to accepted and confirmed registrants.

Eligibility: Open to all students with a major in the College of Letters & Science.

Priority: Registration priority is given to Seniors (4th years) and students graduating in Spring or Summer 2026. Other students in the College of Letters and Science are encouraged to register and will be admitted as space permits.

title: NAMI Connection Support Group for Young Adults - Virtual
location: CA
description:
NAMI Connection Support Group for Young Adults is a free, peer-led support group for anyone between the ages 18 and 26 who has experienced symptoms of a mental health condition. Mental health conditions include anxiety disorder, depression, bipolar disorder, obsessive-compulsive disorder, post-traumatic stress disorder, schizophrenia, or any condition that affects a person’s ability to lead a fulfilling life. You do not need a formal diagnosis to attend this support group; any young person concerned with their mental health and seeking support may attend. NAMI Connection for Young Adults provides resources to help young people build a support network and access help. Participants will learn together how to establish and maintain wellness. 
You do not need a formal diagnosis to attend. Anyone who is seeking mental health support may attend. 
What You’ll Gain
By sharing your experiences in a safe and confidential setting, you will experience connection, receive information about resources, and learn coping strategies. The group encourages empathy, productive discussion and a sense of community. You’ll benefit from other’s experiences, discover your inner strength, and receive support by sharing your own experiences in a non-judgmental space. 
  
NAMI Connection Support Group for Young Adults meets on the first Wednesday of the month (in person) and on the third Wednesday of the month  (via Zoom) from 7pm-8pm.  
To register for in-person or virtual meetings, please visit: https://namiyolo.org/support-and-education/nami-yolo-county-support-groups/young-adult-connection-support-group/. 
If you have any questions, please email: friends@namiyolo.org.
